query: heavy lens flare sun glare washout veil photograph haze
message: anti_aesthetics/lighting: lens flare overload — sun glare, washout, hexagonal flare artifacts, veiled hazy aesthetic

response.objective_caption: A low-angle view looking up at the corner of a concrete building against a bright yellow sky. The sun sits directly at the edge of the roofline, producing bright lens flare. The building exterior consists of large rectangular panels with weathered markings, and a thin cable runs along the angled roof edge.
response.anti_aesthetic_caption: A low-angle view looking up at the corner of a concrete building against a bright yellow sky, framed with a steep diagonal tilt that creates an unstable, disorienting composition. The sun is pushed to extremes of overexposure at the roofline, creating blown-out highlights and lens flare that reduce the scene to graphic luminosity. A sickly amber-yellow cast permeates the entire image, tinting the concrete surfaces with an unnatural, unsettling hue reminiscent of sodium vapor light or atmospheric contamination.
